content.problemStatement:
You are given a chain of matrices
A1,A2,A3,…,An−1A_1, A_2, A_3, \ldots, A_{n-1}A1​,A2​,A3​,…,An−1​.

An array nums\texttt{nums}nums of size nnn. The dimension of matrix AiA_iAi​ (1≤i<n)(1 \le i < n)(1≤i<n) is nums[i−1]×nums[i]\texttt{nums}[i-1]\times\texttt{nums}[i]nums[i−1]×nums[i].

The task is to determine the minimum number of scalar multiplications required to multiply the entire chain of matrices by choosing the most optimal placement of parentheses.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT (1≤T≤103)(1 \le T \le 10^3)(1≤T≤103), denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines:

The first line contains a single integer nnn — the size of the array nums\texttt{nums}nums.
The second line contains nnn space-separated integers nums0,nums1,…,numsn−1\texttt{nums}_0, \texttt{nums}_1, \ldots, \texttt{nums}_{n-1}nums0​,nums1​,…,numsn−1​.




Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer — the minimum number of multiplications needed to multiply the given chain of matrices.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
A1 -> 10 × 15
A2 -> 15 × 20
A3 -> 20 × 25
Possible parenthesizations:
A1 × (A2 × A3)
Cost = 15 × 20 × 25 + 10 × 15 × 25 = 11250
(A1 × A2) × A3
Cost =  10 × 15 × 20 + 10 × 20 × 25 = 8000
Minimum cost = 8000

Explanation:
A1 -> 5 × 4
A2 -> 4 × 6
A3 -> 6 × 2
A4 -> 2 × 7
One optimal parenthesization:
(A1 × (A2 × A3)) × A4
Cost: A2 × A3 -> 4 × 6 × 2 = 48
A1 × result -> 5 × 4 × 2 = 40
Result × A4 -> 5 × 2 × 7 = 70
Total = 158

code.Java:
class Solution {
    public int matrixMultiplication(int[] nums) {                     // function to compute minimum scalar multiplications
        int n = nums.length;                                          // total number of dimension entries
        int m = n - 1;                                                // total number of matrices

        if (m <= 0) return 0;                                         // if no matrix exists, return 0

        long[][] dp = new long[m][m];                                 // dp[i][j] stores minimum cost to multiply matrices i..j

        for (int len = 2; len <= m; len++) {                          // len is the current chain length (number of matrices)
            for (int i = 0; i + len - 1 < m; i++) {                   // starting matrix index
                int j = i + len - 1;                                  // ending matrix index
                long best = Long.MAX_VALUE;                           // store minimum cost for this segment

                for (int k = i; k < j; k++) {                         // try all possible split points
                    long cost = dp[i][k]                              // cost to multiply left subchain
                              + dp[k + 1][j]                          // cost to multiply right subchain
                              + 1L * nums[i]                          // rows of left result matrix
                              * nums[k + 1]                           // common dimension
                              * nums[j + 1];                          // columns of right result matrix

                    if (cost < best)                                  // check if current split gives smaller cost
                        best = cost;                                  // update best cost
                }

                dp[i][j] = best;                                      // store the minimum cost for matrices i..j
            }
        }

        return (int) dp[0][m - 1];                                     // return minimum cost to multiply all matrices
    }
}
